Assembly: On Nov 19, 2025 this sequence version replaced LS420026.1.; The assembly fAstCal1.5 is based on ~48x PacBio RSII data, ~112x Illumina HiSeqX data generated from a 10X Genomics Chromium library, and single-enzyme BioNano Irys data. Hi-C was obtained from sperm of an A. calliptera individual from 'Mbenji Island? in Lake Malawi. An initial PacBio assembly was made using Falcon-unzip and the primary contigs extended by merging with a miniasm assembly. The contigs were then scaffolded using the 10X data with scaff10x. After using the PacBio data to gap fill with PBJelly and polish with Quiver, the assembly was manually curated using gEVAL to correct mis-joins and improve concordance with the BioNano data. The assembly was then polished again using the 10X Illumina data and freebayes. Two genetic maps [PMID: 23622422, 25156298] were used to allocate scaffolds to chromosomes, followed by further curation and verification using gEVAL. The Hi-C data was used for manual curation using Pretext View. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP.

Assembly: The assembly TroMau2.2 is based on ~133x PacBio CLR data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme~(https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/) and Arima2 Hi-C data generated at the University of Bern. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: PacBio CLR data assembled with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named according to standard Malawi cichlid nomenclature.

Assembly: The assembly fAulStu2.1 is based on 18x ONT duplex data generated at the University of Cambridge and Arima2 Hi-C data generated at the University of Bern. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: ONT duplex reads assembled with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named according to standard Malawi cichlid nomenclature.

Assembly: The assembly fDipLim2.hap1.1 is based on 86x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (0 - 18 Mbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 11 (36 - 38.5 Mbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 14 (30 - 33 Mbp) are unknown. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.

Assembly: The assembly fAstCal68 is based on 65x ONT simplex data generated at the University of Cambridge and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme~(https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial ONT error correction with herro, then assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named according to standard Malawi cichlid nomenclature.

Assembly: On Jul 23, 2024 this sequence version replaced OY755037.1.; The fNeoMul1.2 assembly is based on 49x PacBio Sequel data together with 66x coverage 10X Genomics linked read library from one male individual. It was assembled using Falcon-unzip, followed by removal of haplotypic duplication with purge_dups. The assembled contigs were polished using the long reads with Arrow, followed by two rounds of polishing with the linked reads with freebayes and bcftools consensus. The scaffolds were placed onto chromosomes with YaHs (https://doi.org/10.1101/2022.06.09.495093) using 163x coverage Hi-C data. The chromosomes were manually ordered and oriented to match the chromosome order and orientation of the A. calliptera genome (fAstCal1.3; GCA_900246225.5). Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL.

Assembly: The assembly fRhaChi2.1 is based on 64x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Errantia; Phyllodocida; Polynoidae; Halosydna
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Errantia; Phyllodocida; Polynoidae; Halosydna
Assembly: The assembly wpHalBrev1.1 is based on 25x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. There are haplotypic inversions on Chromosome 5 from 0-~44.68Mb, Chromosome 15 from ~16.28-40.43Mb, Chromosome 12 from ~12.32-49.50Mb and Chromosome 17 from ~10.30-37.07Mb. Order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 2 from ~12.42-17.20Mb, Chromosome 17 from ~44.08-46.95Mb is unsure.
